Default 4. 171: 172: Attributes 173: ---------- 174: config : dict[str, Any] 175: Configuration dictionary. 176: device : torch.device 177: Computation device. 178: encoder : T5EncoderModel 179: Pre-trained T5 encoder. 180: tokenizer : T5TokenizerFast 181: Fast T5 tokenizer. 182: policy_head : PolicyHead 183: Custom three-head policy module. 184: max_input_length : int 185: Maximum token sequence length for tokenization. 186: 187: Examples 188: -------- 189: >>> config = {"model_name": "t5-small", "device": "cpu", "num_choices": 4} 190: >>> model = T5PolicyModel(config) 191: >>> texts = ["CLUES: first president | CHOICES: (1) Washington (2) Jefferson"] 192: >>> wait_logits, answer_logits, values = model(texts) 193: >>> wait_logits.shape 194: torch.Size([1, 2]) 195: """ 196: 197: def __init__(self, config: Dict[str, Any]) -> None: 198: super().__init__() 199: from transformers import T5EncoderModel, T5TokenizerFast 200: 201: self.config = config 202: model_name = config.get("model_name", "t5-large") 203: self.max_input_length = config.get("max_input_length", 512) 204: num_choices = config.get("num_choices", 4) 205: 206: # Auto-detect device 207: default_device = "cpu" 208: if torch.cuda.is_available(): 209: default_device = "cuda" 210: elif hasattr(torch.backends, "mps") and torch.backends.mps.is_available(): 211: default_device = "mps" 212: self.device = torch.device(config.get("device", default_device)) 213: 214: # Load T5 encoder only (not full T5ForConditionalGeneration) 215: # This is 2x faster and uses 50% less memory since the decoder is unused 216: print(f"Loading T5 encoder: {model_name}") 217: self.encoder = T5EncoderModel.from_pretrained(model_name) 218: self.tokenizer = T5TokenizerFast.from_pretrained(model_name) 219: 220: # Get hidden size from T5 config (512 for small, 768 for base, 1024 for large) 221: hidden_size = self.encoder.config.d_model 222: 223: # Custom policy head 224: self.policy_head = PolicyHead( 225: hidden_size=hidden_size, 226: num_choices=num_choices, 227: ) 228: 229: # Move to device 230: self.to(self.device) 231: 232: # Print model info 233: self._print_model_info() 234: 235: def _print_model_info(self) -> None: 236: """Print model architecture summary and parameter counts.""" 237: encoder_params = sum(p.numel() for p in self.encoder.parameters()) 238: policy_params = sum(p.numel() for p in self.policy_head.parameters()) 239: total_params = encoder_params + policy_params 240: 241: print(f"Model Architecture:") 242: print(f" T5 encoder parameters: {encoder_params:,}") 243: print(f" Policy head parameters: {policy_params:,}") 244: print(f" Total parameters: {total_params:,}") 245: print(f" Device: {self.device}") 246: 247: def encode_input( 248: self, 249: text_inputs: List[str], 250: max_length: Optional[int] = None, 251: ) -> Dict[str, torch.Tensor]: 252: """Tokenize text inputs using T5TokenizerFast. 253: 254: Parameters 255: ---------- 256: text_inputs : list[str] 257: List of input text strings to tokenize. 258: max_length : int or None 259: Maximum sequence length. If None, uses ``self.max_input_length``. 260: 261: Returns 262: ------- 263: dict[str, torch.Tensor] 264: Dictionary with ``"input_ids"`` and ``"attention_mask"`` tensors, 265: both of shape ``[batch_size, seq_len]``, moved to ``self.device``. 266: """ 267: if max_length is None: 268: max_length = self.max_input_length 269: 270: encoding = self.tokenizer( 271: text_inputs, 272: padding=True, 273: truncation=True, 274: max_length=max_length, 275: return_tensors="pt", 276: ) 277: 278: return {k: v.to(self.device) for k, v in encoding.items()} 279: 280: def get_encoder_output( 281: self, 282: input_ids: torch.Tensor, 283: attention_mask: torch.Tensor, 284: ) -> torch.Tensor: 285: """Compute T5 encoder output and pool to a fixed-size vector. 286: 287: Uses attention-masked mean pooling: sum hidden states where attention 288: mask is 1, divide by number of non-padding tokens. This ensures 289: padding tokens contribute zero to the pooled representation. 290: 291: Parameters 292: ---------- 293: input_ids : torch.Tensor 294: Token IDs of shape ``[batch_size, seq_len]``. 295: attention_mask : torch.Tensor 296: Attention mask of shape ``[batch_size, seq_len]`` (1 for real 297: tokens, 0 for padding). 298: 299: Returns 300: ------- 301: torch.Tensor 302: Pooled encoder output of shape ``[batch_size, hidden_size]``. 303: """ 304: # Get encoder outputs 305: encoder_outputs = self.encoder( 306: input_ids=input_ids, 307: attention_mask=attention_mask, 308: return_dict=True, 309: ) 310: 311: # encoder_outputs.last_hidden_state: [batch_size, seq_len, hidden_size] 312: hidden_states = encoder_outputs.last_hidden_state 313: 314: # Attention-masked mean pooling over sequence dimension 315: mask_expanded = attention_mask.unsqueeze(-1).expand(hidden_states.size()).float() 316: sum_hidden = torch.sum(hidden_states * mask_expanded, dim=1) 317: sum_mask = torch.clamp(mask_expanded.sum(dim=1), min=1e-9) 318: pooled_output = sum_hidden / sum_mask 319: 320: return pooled_output 321: 322: def forward( 323: self, 324: text_inputs: List[str], 325: return_value: bool = True, 326: ) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor, Optional[torch.Tensor]]: 327: """Forward pass: tokenize, encode, pool, then apply policy head. 328: 329: Parameters 330: ---------- 331: text_inputs : list[str] 332: List of text observations (e.g., 333: ``"CLUES: clue1 clue2 | CHOICES: (1) ans1 (2) ans2"``). 334: return_value : bool 335: If True, return value estimates. If False, values is None. 336: 337: Returns 338: ------- 339: wait_logits : torch.Tensor 340: Shape ``[batch_size, 2]`` -- logits for [wait, answer_now]. 341: answer_logits : torch.Tensor 342: Shape ``[batch_size, num_choices]`` -- logits over answer options. 343: values : torch.Tensor or None 344: Shape ``[batch_size, 1]`` if return_value is True, else None. 345: """ 346: # Encode inputs 347: encoding = self.encode_input(text_inputs) 348: 349: # Get pooled encoder output 350: pooled_output = self.get_encoder_output( 351: encoding["input_ids"], 352: encoding["attention_mask"], 353: ) 354: 355: # Pass through policy head 356: wait_logits, answer_logits, values = self.policy_head(pooled_output) 357: 358: if not return_value: 359: values = None 360: 361: return wait_logits, answer_logits, values 362: 363: def predict_answer( 364: self, 365: input_ids: torch.Tensor, 366: attention_mask: torch.Tensor, 367: ) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]: 368: """Predict answer choice for supervised training. 369: 370: Only uses the answer head (wait and value heads are ignored). This is 371: the interface for supervised warm-start training where the model learns 372: to select the correct answer from complete questions. 373: 374: Parameters 375: ---------- 376: input_ids : torch.Tensor 377: Token IDs of shape ``[batch_size, seq_len]``. 378: attention_mask : torch.Tensor 379: Attention mask of shape ``[batch_size, seq_len]``. 380: 381: Returns 382: ------- 383: answer_logits : torch.Tensor 384: Shape ``[batch_size, num_choices]`` -- logits over answer choices. 385: predictions : torch.Tensor 386: Shape ``[batch_size]`` -- predicted answer indices (argmax). 387: """ 388: # Get encoder output 389: pooled_output = self.get_encoder_output(input_ids, attention_mask) 390: 391: # Get answer logits from policy head 392: _, answer_logits, _ = self.policy_head(pooled_output) 393: 394: # Get predictions 395: predictions = torch.argmax(answer_logits, dim=-1) 396: 397: return answer_logits, predictions 398: 399: def _joint_action_log_prob( 400: self, 401: wait_logits: torch.Tensor, 402: answer_logits: torch.Tensor, 403: actions: torch.Tensor, 404: ) -> torch.Tensor: 405: """Compute factorized log-probabilities for flat WAIT/BUZZ actions. 406: 407: Parameters 408: ---------- 409: wait_logits : torch.Tensor 410: Binary logits of shape ``[batch_size, 2]`` for [WAIT, BUZZ]. 411: answer_logits : torch.Tensor 412: Answer logits of shape ``[batch_size, K]``. 413: actions : torch.Tensor 414: Flat actions of shape ``[batch_size]`` where 0 = WAIT and 415: 1..K = BUZZ with answer index action-1. 416: 417: Returns 418: ------- 419: torch.Tensor 420: Log-probabilities of shape ``[batch_size]``. 421: """ 422: wait_log_probs = F.log_softmax(wait_logits, dim=-1) 423: answer_log_probs = F.log_softmax(answer_logits, dim=-1) 424: 425: wait_actions = (actions > 0).long() 426: answer_actions = torch.clamp(actions - 1, min=0) 427: 428: selected_wait = wait_log_probs.gather(1, wait_actions.unsqueeze(-1)).squeeze(-1) 429: selected_answer = answer_log_probs.gather( 430: 1, answer_actions.unsqueeze(-1) 431: ).squeeze(-1) 432: 433: return torch.where(actions == 0, selected_wait, selected_wait + selected_answer) 434: 435: def _joint_entropy( 436: self, 437: wait_logits: torch.Tensor, 438: answer_logits: torch.Tensor, 439: ) -> torch.Tensor: 440: """Compute chain-rule entropy for the factorized wait/answer policy. 441: 442: Returns ``H(wait) + p_buzz * H(answer)`` for each example. 443: """ 444: wait_probs = F.softmax(wait_logits, dim=-1) 445: wait_log_probs = F.log_softmax(wait_logits, dim=-1) 446: answer_probs = F.softmax(answer_logits, dim=-1) 447: answer_log_probs = F.log_softmax(answer_logits, dim=-1) 448: 449: wait_entropy = -(wait_probs * wait_log_probs).sum(dim=-1) 450: answer_entropy = -(answer_probs * answer_log_probs).sum(dim=-1) 451: return wait_entropy + wait_probs[:, 1] * answer_entropy 452: 453: def select_action( 454: self, 455: input_ids: torch.Tensor, 456: attention_mask: torch.Tensor, 457: deterministic: bool = False, 458: temperature: float = 1.0, 459: ) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, Dict[str, Any]]: 460: """Select flat WAIT/BUZZ actions from the factorized policy. 461: 462: Produces combined actions following TossupMCEnv convention: 463: 0 = WAIT, 1..K = SELECT answer 0..K-1. Under the factorized policy: 464: 465: - ``P(WAIT) = p_wait`` 466: - ``P(BUZZ_i) = p_buzz * p_ans(i)`` 467: 468: Answer sampling only occurs for examples that actually buzz. 469: 470: Parameters 471: ---------- 472: input_ids : torch.Tensor 473: Token IDs of shape ``[batch_size, seq_len]``. 474: attention_mask : torch.Tensor 475: Attention mask of shape ``[batch_size, seq_len]``. 476: deterministic : bool 477: If True, use argmax instead of sampling. 478: temperature : float 479: Temperature for softmax. Higher values increase randomness. 480: Default 1.0 (no scaling). 481: 482: Returns 483: ------- 484: combined_actions : torch.Tensor 485: Shape ``[batch_size]`` -- combined actions (0 = WAIT, 1..K = SELECT). 486: info : dict[str, Any] 487: Dictionary with keys: 488: 489: - ``wait_logits``: raw wait head output 490: - ``answer_logits``: raw answer head output 491: - ``wait_probs``: softmax of wait logits 492: - ``answer_probs``: softmax of answer logits 493: - ``wait_actions``: sampled wait decisions (0 or 1) 494: - ``answer_actions``: sampled answer indices (0..K-1) 495: - ``values``: value estimates 496: - ``log_probs``: total log probability of the combined action 497: """ 498: with torch.no_grad(): 499: pooled_output = self.get_encoder_output(input_ids, attention_mask) 500: wait_logits, answer_logits, values = self.policy_head(pooled_output) 501: 502: wait_logits_scaled = wait_logits / temperature 503: answer_logits_scaled = answer_logits / temperature 504: 505: wait_probs = F.softmax(wait_logits_scaled, dim=-1) 506: answer_probs = F.softmax(answer_logits_scaled, dim=-1) 507: flat_action_probs = torch.cat( 508: [wait_probs[:, :1], wait_probs[:, 1:2] * answer_probs], 509: dim=-1, 510: ) 511: 512: if deterministic: 513: combined_actions = torch.argmax(flat_action_probs, dim=-1) 514: wait_actions = (combined_actions > 0).long() 515: answer_actions = torch.clamp(combined_actions - 1, min=0) 516: else: 517: wait_actions = torch.distributions.Categorical(wait_probs).sample() 518: answer_actions = torch.argmax(answer_probs, dim=-1) 519: buzz_mask = wait_actions == 1 520: if buzz_mask.any(): 521: buzz_answers = torch.distributions.Categorical( 522: answer_probs[buzz_mask] 523: ).sample() 524: answer_actions = answer_actions.clone() 525: answer_actions[buzz_mask] = buzz_answers 526: combined_actions = torch.where( 527: wait_actions == 0, 528: torch.zeros_like(wait_actions), 529: 1 + answer_actions, 530: ) 531: 532: log_probs = self._joint_action_log_prob( 533: wait_logits_scaled, answer_logits_scaled, combined_actions 534: ) 535: 536: combined_actions = torch.where( 537: wait_actions == 0, 538: torch.zeros_like(wait_actions), 539: 1 + answer_actions, 540: ) 541: 542: info = { 543: "wait_logits": wait_logits, 544: "answer_logits": answer_logits, 545: "wait_probs": wait_probs, 546: "answer_probs": answer_probs, 547: "wait_actions": wait_actions, 548: "answer_actions": answer_actions, 549: "values": values, 550: "log_probs": log_probs, 551: } 552: 553: return combined_actions, info 554: 555: def get_action_log_probs( 556: self, 557: input_ids: torch.Tensor, 558: attention_mask: torch.Tensor, 559: actions: torch.Tensor, 560: ) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]: 561: """Compute log probabilities and entropy for given actions. 562: 563: Used during PPO training to evaluate old actions under the current 564: policy. Combined actions follow the factorized semantics: 565: 566: - ``P(WAIT) = p_wait`` 567: - ``P(BUZZ_i) = p_buzz * p_ans(i)`` 568: 569: Parameters 570: ---------- 571: input_ids : torch.Tensor 572: Token IDs of shape ``[batch_size, seq_len]``. 573: attention_mask : torch.Tensor 574: Attention mask of shape ``[batch_size, seq_len]``. 575: actions : torch.Tensor 576: Combined actions of shape ``[batch_size]``. Values in {0, 1, ..., K}. 577: 578: Returns 579: ------- 580: log_probs : torch.Tensor 581: Shape ``[batch_size]`` -- total log probability of each action. 582: entropy : torch.Tensor 583: Shape ``[batch_size]`` -- chain-rule entropy for the factorized policy. 584: values : torch.Tensor 585: Shape ``[batch_size]`` -- value estimates (squeezed). 586: """ 587: pooled_output = self.get_encoder_output(input_ids, attention_mask) 588: wait_logits, answer_logits, values = self.policy_head(pooled_output) 589: 590: log_probs = self._joint_action_log_prob(wait_logits, answer_logits, actions) 591: entropy = self._joint_entropy(wait_logits, answer_logits) 592: 593: return log_probs, entropy, values.squeeze(-1) 594: 595: def save(self, save_dir: str) -> None: 596: """Save model checkpoint to disk. 597: 598: Saves three components: 599: 1. T5 encoder weights and config (HuggingFace format) 600: 2. Tokenizer files (HuggingFace format) 601: 3. Policy head state dict (PyTorch format as ``policy_head.pt``) 602: 603: Parameters 604: ---------- 605: save_dir : str 606: Directory path to save the checkpoint. Created if it doesn't exist. 607: """ 608: os.makedirs(save_dir, exist_ok=True) 609: 610: # Save T5 encoder 611: self.encoder.save_pretrained(save_dir) 612: self.tokenizer.save_pretrained(save_dir) 613: 614: # Save policy head 615: policy_head_path = os.path.join(save_dir, "policy_head.pt") 616: torch.save(self.policy_head.state_dict(), policy_head_path) 617: 618: print(f"Model saved to {save_dir}") 619: 620: def load(self, load_dir: str) -> None: 621: """Load model checkpoint from disk. 622: 623: Loads T5 encoder weights, tokenizer, and policy head state dict from 624: the specified directory. The model is moved to ``self.device`` after 625: loading. 626: 627: Parameters 628: ---------- 629: load_dir : str 630: Directory containing a previously saved checkpoint. 631: 632: Raises 633: ------ 634: FileNotFoundError 635: If ``policy_head.pt`` is not found in ``load_dir``. 636: """ 637: from transformers import T5EncoderModel, T5TokenizerFast 638: 639: # Load T5 encoder 640: self.encoder = T5EncoderModel.from_pretrained(load_dir) 641: self.tokenizer = T5TokenizerFast.from_pretrained(load_dir) 642: 643: # Load policy head 644: policy_head_path = os.path.join(load_dir, "policy_head.pt") 645: self.policy_head.load_state_dict( 646: torch.load(policy_head_path, map_location=self.device, weights_only=True) 647: ) 648: 649: self.to(self.device) 650: print(f"Model loaded from {load_dir}") 651: 652: @classmethod 653: def load_pretrained( 654: cls, 655: load_dir: str, 656: device: Optional[str] = None, 657: ) -> "T5PolicyModel": 658: """Load a pretrained model from a directory. 659: 660: Class method that creates a new T5PolicyModel instance and loads 661: weights from a saved checkpoint. 662: 663: Parameters 664: ---------- 665: load_dir : str 666: Directory containing a previously saved checkpoint. 667: device : str or None 668: Device to load model on (e.g., ``"cpu"``, ``"cuda"``, ``"mps"``). 669: If None, auto-detects. 670: 671: Returns 672: ------- 673: T5PolicyModel 674: A loaded model instance ready for inference. 675: """ 676: from transformers import T5EncoderModel 677: 678: # Detect model config from saved checkpoint 679: t5_encoder = T5EncoderModel.from_pretrained(load_dir, local_files_only=True) 680: hidden_size = t5_encoder.config.d_model 681: 682: # Infer num_choices from policy head state dict 683: policy_head_path = os.path.join(load_dir, "policy_head.pt") 684: policy_head_state = torch.load( 685: policy_head_path, map_location="cpu", weights_only=True 686: ) 687: # answer_head final linear layer weight shape is [num_choices, hidden_dim] 688: num_choices = policy_head_state["answer_head.3.weight"].shape[0] 689: 690: config = { 691: "model_name": load_dir, 692: "device": device or "cpu", 693: "num_choices": num_choices, 694: } 695: 696: model = cls(config) 697: model.load(load_dir) 698: return model 1: """Answer profile builder with leave-one-out exclusion for quiz bowl questions.""" 2: 3: from __future__ import annotations 4: 5: from collections import defaultdict 6: from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple 7: 8: from qb_data.data_loader import TossupQuestion 9: 10: 11: class AnswerProfileBuilder: 12: """Builds profiles for answers by aggregating question texts. 13: 14: The profile for an answer is created by concatenating all question texts 15: that have that answer. When building profiles for distractors, we use 16: all questions. For the gold answer, we exclude the current question to 17: prevent information leakage (leave-one-out). 18: 19: Attributes: 20: max_tokens_per_profile: Maximum number of tokens to keep in each profile. 21: min_questions_per_answer: Minimum questions needed to build a profile. 22: _grouped: Dictionary mapping answer_primary to list of (qid, question_text) tuples. 23: """ 24: 25: def __init__( 26: self, 27: max_tokens_per_profile: int = 2000, 28: min_questions_per_answer: int = 1 29: ): 30: """Initialize the answer profile builder. 31: 32: Args: 33: max_tokens_per_profile: Maximum tokens to keep in each profile. 34: min_questions_per_answer: Minimum questions needed to build a profile. 35: """ 36: self.max_tokens_per_profile = max_tokens_per_profile 37: self.min_questions_per_answer = min_questions_per_answer 38: self._grouped: Dict[str, List[Tuple[str, str]]] = {} 39: self._cache: Dict[Tuple[str, Optional[str]], str] = {} 40: 41: def fit(self, questions: List[TossupQuestion]) -> "AnswerProfileBuilder": 42: """Fit the builder on a set of questions. 43: 44: Groups questions by their primary answer for efficient profile building. 45: 46: Args: 47: questions: List of tossup questions to group by answer. 48: 49: Returns: 50: Self for method chaining. 51: """ 52: grouped: Dict[str, List[Tuple[str, str]]] = defaultdict(list) 53: for q in questions: 54: # Store qid and full question text for each answer 55: grouped[q.answer_primary].append((q.qid, q.question)) 56: self._grouped = dict(grouped) 57: self._cache = {} 58: return self 59: 60: def _profile_text( 61: self, 62: answer_primary: str, 63: exclude_qid: Optional[str] = None 64: ) -> str: 65: """Build profile text for an answer with optional exclusion. 66: 67: Args: 68: answer_primary: The answer to build a profile for. 69: exclude_qid: Optional question ID to exclude (leave-one-out). 70: 71: Returns: 72: Profile text truncated to max_tokens_per_profile. 73: """ 74: key = (answer_primary, exclude_qid) 75: if key in self._cache: 76: return self._cache[key] 77: 78: items = self._grouped.get(answer_primary, []) 79: texts: List[str] = [] 80: 81: # Collect all question texts except the excluded one 82: for qid, qtext in items: 83: if exclude_qid is not None and qid == exclude_qid: 84: continue 85: texts.append(qtext) 86: 87: # If not enough questions after exclusion, fall back to answer text 88: if len(texts) < self.min_questions_per_answer: 89: self._cache[key] = answer_primary 90: return answer_primary 91: 92: # Merge all texts and split into tokens 93: merged = " ".join(texts).split() 94: 95: # Truncate to max tokens if specified 96: if self.max_tokens_per_profile > 0: 97: merged = merged[:self.max_tokens_per_profile] 98: 99: result = " ".join(merged) if merged else answer_primary 100: self._cache[key] = result 101: return result 102: 103: def profile_for_answer( 104: self, 105: answer_primary: str, 106: exclude_qid: Optional[str] = None 107: ) -> str: 108: """Get the profile for a specific answer. 109: 110: Args: 111: answer_primary: The answer to get a profile for. 112: exclude_qid: Optional question ID to exclude (for gold answer). 113: 114: Returns: 115: Profile text for the answer. 116: """ 117: return self._profile_text( 118: answer_primary=answer_primary, 119: exclude_qid=exclude_qid 120: ) 121: 122: def build_profiles( 123: self, 124: questions: List[TossupQuestion], 125: exclude_qid: Optional[str] = None, 126: ) -> Dict[str, str]: 127: """Build profiles for all answers in the dataset. 128: 129: Args: 130: questions: List of questions (used to fit if not already fitted). 131: exclude_qid: Optional question ID to exclude from all profiles. 132: 133: Returns: 134: Dictionary mapping answer_primary to profile text. 135: """ 136: if not self._grouped: 137: self.fit(questions) 138: 139: return { 140: answer: self._profile_text(answer, exclude_qid=exclude_qid) 141: for answer in self._grouped.keys() 142: } 1: """ 2: Stratified dataset splitting utilities for quiz bowl data. 3: 4: This module provides functions to create train/val/test splits that maintain 5: category distribution across all splits. 6: """ 7: 8: import hashlib 9: import json 10: import random 11: from collections import defaultdict 12: from pathlib import Path 13: from typing import List, Tuple, Optional, Dict, Any 14: 15: from qb_data.data_loader import TossupQuestion 16: 17: 18: def create_stratified_splits( 19: questions: List[TossupQuestion], 20: ratios: List[float] = [0.7, 0.15, 0.15], 21: seed: int = 42 22: ) -> Tuple[List[TossupQuestion], List[TossupQuestion], List[TossupQuestion]]: 23: """ 24: Create stratified train/val/test splits maintaining category distribution. 25: 26: Parameters 27: ---------- 28: questions : List[TossupQuestion] 29: List of questions to split 30: ratios : List[float] 31: Train/val/test split ratios (must sum to 1.0) 32: seed : int 33: Random seed for reproducibility 34: 35: Returns 36: ------- 37: Tuple[List[TossupQuestion], List[TossupQuestion], List[TossupQuestion]] 38: Train, validation, and test splits 39: 40: Raises 41: ------ 42: ValueError 43: If ratios don't sum to 1.0 or questions list is empty 44: """ 45: # Validate inputs 46: if not questions: 47: raise ValueError("Cannot split empty question list") 48: 49: if abs(sum(ratios) - 1.0) > 1e-6: 50: raise ValueError(f"Ratios must sum to 1.0, got {sum(ratios)}") 51: 52: # Initialize random generator for reproducibility 53: rng = random.Random(seed) 54: 55: # Group questions by category 56: category_groups = defaultdict(list) 57: for q in questions: 58: category_groups[q.category].append(q) 59: 60: # Initialize output lists 61: train_questions = [] 62: val_questions = [] 63: test_questions = [] 64: 65: # Split each category maintaining ratios 66: for category, category_questions in category_groups.items(): 67: # Sort for deterministic splits 68: sorted_questions = sorted(category_questions, key=lambda q: q.qid) 69: 70: # Deterministic per-category seed via MD5 (immune to PYTHONHASHSEED) 71: cat_hash = int(hashlib.md5(category.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest(), 16) 72: category_seed = seed + cat_hash % 1_000_000 73: category_rng = random.Random(category_seed) 74: shuffled = sorted_questions.copy() 75: category_rng.shuffle(shuffled) 76: 77: n = len(shuffled) 78: 79: # Calculate split indices 80: train_end = int(n * ratios[0]) 81: val_end = train_end + int(n * ratios[1]) 82: 83: # Handle small categories - ensure at least 1 in train if possible 84: if n == 1: 85: train_questions.extend(shuffled) 86: elif n == 2: 87: train_questions.extend(shuffled[:1]) 88: val_questions.extend(shuffled[1:]) 89: else: 90: # Standard split 91: train_questions.extend(shuffled[:train_end]) 92: val_questions.extend(shuffled[train_end:val_end]) 93: test_questions.extend(shuffled[val_end:]) 94: 95: # Verify all questions assigned exactly once 96: total_original = len(questions) 97: total_split = len(train_questions) + len(val_questions) + len(test_questions) 98: 99: if total_original != total_split: 100: raise RuntimeError(f"Split mismatch: {total_original} original vs {total_split} split") 101: 102: # Log category distribution statistics 103: print(f"Dataset split complete:") 104: print(f" Train: {len(train_questions)} questions ({len(train_questions)/total_original:.1%})") 105: print(f" Val: {len(val_questions)} questions ({len(val_questions)/total_original:.1%})") 106: print(f" Test: {len(test_questions)} questions ({len(test_questions)/total_original:.1%})") 107: 108: # Category distribution analysis 109: train_categories = defaultdict(int) 110: val_categories = defaultdict(int) 111: test_categories = defaultdict(int) 112: 113: for q in train_questions: 114: train_categories[q.category] += 1 115: for q in val_questions: 116: val_categories[q.category] += 1 117: for q in test_questions: 118: test_categories[q.category] += 1 119: 120: all_categories = set(train_categories.keys()) | set(val_categories.keys()) | set(test_categories.keys()) 121: print(f"\nCategory distribution ({len(all_categories)} categories):") 122: 123: for category in sorted(all_categories)[:5]: # Show first 5 categories 124: orig_count = len(category_groups[category]) 125: train_count = train_categories.get(category, 0) 126: val_count = val_categories.get(category, 0) 127: test_count = test_categories.get(category, 0) 128: print(f" {category}: {train_count}/{val_count}/{test_count} (orig: {orig_count})") 129: 130: if len(all_categories) > 5: 131: print(f" ... and {len(all_categories) - 5} more categories") 132: 133: return train_questions, val_questions, test_questions 134: 135: 136: def save_splits( 137: train: List[TossupQuestion], 138: val: List[TossupQuestion], 139: test: List[TossupQuestion], 140: output_dir: str = "data" 141: ) -> None: 142: """ 143: Save dataset splits to JSON files with metadata. 144: 145: Parameters 146: ---------- 147: train : List[TossupQuestion] 148: Training split 149: val : List[TossupQuestion] 150: Validation split 151: test : List[TossupQuestion] 152: Test split 153: output_dir : str 154: Directory to save split files 155: """ 156: output_path = Path(output_dir) 157: output_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) 158: 159: # Helper to convert TossupQuestion to dict 160: def questions_to_dict(questions: List[TossupQuestion]) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]: 161: return [ 162: { 163: "qid": q.qid, 164: "question": q.question, 165: "tokens": q.tokens, 166: "answer_primary": q.answer_primary, 167: "clean_answers": q.clean_answers, 168: "run_indices": q.run_indices, 169: "human_buzz_positions": q.human_buzz_positions, 170: "category": q.category, 171: "cumulative_prefixes": q.cumulative_prefixes 172: } 173: for q in questions 174: ] 175: 176: # Calculate category distributions for metadata 177: def get_category_distribution(questions: List[TossupQuestion]) -> Dict[str, int]: 178: dist = defaultdict(int) 179: for q in questions: 180: dist[q.category] += 1 181: return dict(dist) 182: 183: # Save each split with metadata 184: splits = [ 185: ("train_dataset.json", train), 186: ("val_dataset.json", val), 187: ("test_dataset.json", test) 188: ] 189: 190: for filename, questions in splits: 191: filepath = output_path / filename 192: 193: data = { 194: "metadata": { 195: "total_questions": len(questions), 196: "categories": len(set(q.category for q in questions)), 197: "category_distribution": get_category_distribution(questions), 198: "split_type": filename.replace("_dataset.json", "") 199: }, 200: "questions": questions_to_dict(questions) 201: } 202: 203: with open(filepath, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f: 204: json.dump(data, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) 205: 206: print(f"Saved {len(questions)} questions to {filepath}") 207: 208: # Save combined metadata file 209: metadata_path = output_path / "split_metadata.json" 210: metadata = { 211: "train": { 212: "count": len(train), 213: "categories": get_category_distribution(train) 214: }, 215: "val": { 216: "count": len(val), 217: "categories": get_category_distribution(val) 218: }, 219: "test": { 220: "count": len(test), 221: "categories": get_category_distribution(test) 222: }, 223: "total_questions": len(train) + len(val) + len(test), 224: "split_ratios": [ 225: len(train) / (len(train) + len(val) + len(test)), 226: len(val) / (len(train) + len(val) + len(test)), 227: len(test) / (len(train) + len(val) + len(test)) 228: ] 229: } 230: 231: with open(metadata_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f: 232: json.dump(metadata, f, indent=2) 233: 234: print(f"\nSaved split metadata to {metadata_path}") 1: """Quiz Bowl Environment Package. 2: 3: Gymnasium-compliant POMDP environment for quiz bowl question answering, 4: plus thin qb-rl compatibility exports for the old `qb_env.*` import paths. 5: """ 6: 7: from qb_env.data_loader import ( 8: QANTADatasetLoader, 9: TossupQuestion, 10: load_tossup_questions, 11: load_tossup_questions_from_config, 12: parse_row, 13: ) 14: from qb_env.mc_builder import MCBuilder, MCQuestion 15: from qb_env.stop_only_env import StopOnlyEnv 16: from qb_env.text_utils import normalize_answer, tokenize_text 17: from qb_env.tossup_env import TossupMCEnv, make_env_from_config 18: from qb_env.text_wrapper import TextObservationWrapper 19: 20: __all__ = [ 21: "TossupMCEnv", 22: "make_env_from_config", 23: "TextObservationWrapper", 24: "TossupQuestion", 25: "QANTADatasetLoader", 26: "parse_row", 27: "load_tossup_questions", 28: "load_tossup_questions_from_config", 29: "MCQuestion", 30: "MCBuilder", 31: "StopOnlyEnv", 32: "normalize_answer", 33: "tokenize_text", 34: ] 1: """Opponent buzz-position models for Expected Wins reward computation. 2: 3: Provides pluggable opponent models that estimate the probability an 4: opponent has buzzed before a given step. Used by the ``expected_wins`` 5: reward mode in :class:`TossupMCEnv`. 6: 7: Three built-in models: 8: 9: * :class:`EmpiricalHistogramOpponentModel` — derives CDF from 10: ``MCQuestion.human_buzz_positions`` data. 11: * :class:`LogisticOpponentModel` — parametric sigmoid CDF for 12: questions that lack empirical data. 13: * :func:`build_opponent_model_from_config` — factory with fallback 14: hierarchy: question-level empirical → global empirical → logistic. 15: 16: The ``expected_wins`` reward mode is disabled by default. To enable, 17: set ``environment.reward_mode: expected_wins`` and optionally configure 18: ``environment.opponent_buzz_model`` in the YAML config. 19: """ 20: 21: from __future__ import annotations 22: 23: import math 24: from typing import Any, Protocol, runtime_checkable 25: 26: import numpy as np 27: 28: from qb_data.mc_builder import MCQuestion 29: 30: 31: @runtime_checkable 32: class OpponentBuzzModel(Protocol): 33: """Protocol for opponent buzz-position models.""" 34: 35: def prob_buzzed_before_step(self, question: MCQuestion, step_idx: int) -> float: 36: """Cumulative probability that the opponent has buzzed before *step_idx*. 37: 38: Parameters 39: ---------- 40: question : MCQuestion 41: Current question (may carry ``human_buzz_positions``). 42: step_idx : int 43: 0-based clue step. 44: 45: Returns 46: ------- 47: float 48: P(opponent buzzed before step_idx), in [0, 1]. 49: """ 50: ... 51: 52: def prob_survive_to_step(self, question: MCQuestion, step_idx: int) -> float: 53: """Probability that the opponent has NOT buzzed by *step_idx*. 54: 55: Complement of :meth:`prob_buzzed_before_step`. 56: """ 57: ... 58: 59: 60: class LogisticOpponentModel: 61: """Parametric logistic CDF opponent model. 62: 63: Models the opponent's cumulative buzz probability at step *t* as:: 64: 65: P(buzzed before t) = 1 / (1 + exp(-steepness * (t/total - midpoint))) 66: 67: Parameters 68: ---------- 69: midpoint : float 70: Fraction of total steps at which the CDF reaches 0.5. 71: steepness : float 72: Controls how sharply the probability increases around the 73: midpoint. Higher values → sharper transition. 74: """ 75: 76: def __init__(self, midpoint: float = 0.6, steepness: float = 6.0) -> None: 77: self.midpoint = midpoint 78: self.steepness = steepness 79: 80: def prob_buzzed_before_step(self, question: MCQuestion, step_idx: int) -> float: 81: total = len(question.cumulative_prefixes) 82: if total <= 1: 83: return 0.0 84: frac = step_idx / total 85: x = self.steepness * (frac - self.midpoint) 86: if x >= 0: 87: return 1.0 / (1.0 + math.exp(-x)) 88: z = math.exp(x) 89: return z / (1.0 + z) 90: 91: def prob_survive_to_step(self, question: MCQuestion, step_idx: int) -> float: 92: return 1.0 - self.prob_buzzed_before_step(question, step_idx) 93: 94: 95: class EmpiricalHistogramOpponentModel: 96: """Opponent model derived from empirical human buzz-position data. 97: 98: Builds a per-step CDF from the ``human_buzz_positions`` field on 99: each question. Falls back to a :class:`LogisticOpponentModel` 100: when a question has no empirical data. 101: 102: Parameters 103: ---------- 104: fallback : LogisticOpponentModel or None 105: Model to use when a question lacks empirical data. 106: global_positions : list of (int, int) or None 107: Pooled (position, count) pairs from the entire dataset. 108: Used when a question has no per-question data but a global 109: distribution is available. 110: """ 111: 112: def __init__( 113: self, 114: fallback: LogisticOpponentModel | None = None, 115: global_positions: list[tuple[int, int]] | None = None, 116: ) -> None: 117: self.fallback = fallback or LogisticOpponentModel() 118: self._global_cdf: np.ndarray | None = None 119: if global_positions: 120: self._global_cdf = self._build_cdf(global_positions) 121: 122: @staticmethod 123: def _build_cdf(positions: list[tuple[int, int]]) -> np.ndarray: 124: """Build a CDF array from (position, count) pairs. 125: 126: Returns an array where ``cdf[i]`` is the cumulative probability 127: that a buzz has occurred at or before position *i*. 128: """ 129: if not positions: 130: return np.array([], dtype=np.float64) 131: max_pos = max(p for p, _ in positions) 132: counts = np.zeros(max_pos + 1, dtype=np.float64) 133: for pos, count in positions: 134: counts[pos] += count 135: total = counts.sum() 136: if total <= 0: 137: return np.zeros(max_pos + 1, dtype=np.float64) 138: return np.cumsum(counts) / total 139: 140: def _cdf_at_step( 141: self, cdf: np.ndarray, question: MCQuestion, step_idx: int 142: ) -> float: 143: """Look up cumulative probability at a token position.""" 144: if cdf.size == 0: 145: return 0.0 146: if not question.run_indices: 147: token_pos = step_idx 148: elif step_idx < len(question.run_indices): 149: token_pos = question.run_indices[step_idx] 150: else: 151: token_pos = question.run_indices[-1] if question.run_indices else step_idx 152: idx = min(token_pos, len(cdf) - 1) 153: return float(cdf[idx]) 154: 155: def prob_buzzed_before_step(self, question: MCQuestion, step_idx: int) -> float: 156: if question.human_buzz_positions: 157: cdf = self._build_cdf(question.human_buzz_positions) 158: return self._cdf_at_step(cdf, question, step_idx) 159: if self._global_cdf is not None and self._global_cdf.size > 0: 160: return self._cdf_at_step(self._global_cdf, question, step_idx) 161: return self.fallback.prob_buzzed_before_step(question, step_idx) 162: 163: def prob_survive_to_step(self, question: MCQuestion, step_idx: int) -> float: 164: return 1.0 - self.prob_buzzed_before_step(question, step_idx) 165: 166: 167: def build_opponent_model_from_config( 168: questions: list[MCQuestion] | None = None, 169: config: dict[str, Any] | None = None, 170: ) -> OpponentBuzzModel | None: 171: """Build an opponent model from YAML configuration. 172: 173: Returns ``None`` when the opponent model is disabled (the default). 174: 175: Parameters 176: ---------- 177: questions : list[MCQuestion] or None 178: Dataset questions for building global empirical distribution. 179: config : dict or None 180: Full YAML config dict. 181: 182: Returns 183: ------- 184: OpponentBuzzModel or None 185: """ 186: if config is None: 187: return None 188: env_cfg = config.get("environment", {}) 189: opp_cfg = env_cfg.get("opponent_buzz_model", {}) 190: if not opp_cfg or opp_cfg.get("type", "none") == "none": 191: return None 192: 193: model_type = opp_cfg.get("type", "logistic") 194: 195: if model_type == "logistic": 196: return LogisticOpponentModel( 197: midpoint=float(opp_cfg.get("midpoint", 0.6)), 198: steepness=float(opp_cfg.get("steepness", 6.0)), 199: ) 200: 201: if model_type == "empirical": 202: global_positions: list[tuple[int, int]] = [] 203: if questions: 204: for q in questions: 205: if q.human_buzz_positions: 206: global_positions.extend(q.human_buzz_positions) 207: fallback = LogisticOpponentModel( 208: midpoint=float(opp_cfg.get("midpoint", 0.6)), 209: steepness=float(opp_cfg.get("steepness", 6.0)), 210: ) 211: return EmpiricalHistogramOpponentModel( 212: fallback=fallback, 213: global_positions=global_positions if global_positions else None, 214: ) 215: 216: raise ValueError(f"Unknown opponent_buzz_model type: {model_type}") 1: """Stop-only action-space wrapper for the quiz bowl environment.""" 2: 3: from __future__ import annotations 4: 5: from typing import Any 6: 7: import gymnasium as gym 8: import numpy as np 9: from gymnasium import spaces 10: 11: from qb_env.tossup_env import TossupMCEnv 12: 13: 14: class StopOnlyEnv(gym.Wrapper): 15: """Wrap TossupMCEnv with a binary WAIT/BUZZ action space. 16: 17: Action mapping: 18: - 0 -> WAIT 19: - 1 -> BUZZ using the current answer-selection strategy 20: 21: The default answer-selection strategy commits to the current belief argmax. 22: """ 23: 24: def __init__(self, env: TossupMCEnv, answer_mode: str = "argmax_belief") -> None: 25: super().__init__(env) 26: self.answer_mode = answer_mode 27: self.action_space = spaces.Discrete(2) 28: 29: def reset( 30: self, 31: *, 32: seed: int | None = None, 33: options: dict[str, Any] | None = None, 34: ) -> tuple[np.ndarray, dict[str, Any]]: 35: return self.env.reset(seed=seed, options=options) 36: 37: def step(self, action: int) -> tuple[np.ndarray, float, bool, bool, dict[str, Any]]: 38: if not self.action_space.contains(action): 39: raise ValueError(f"Invalid action: {action}") 40: if action == 0: 41: return self.env.step(0) 42: 43: if self.answer_mode != "argmax_belief": 44: raise ValueError(f"Unknown answer_mode: {self.answer_mode}") 45: 46: chosen_idx = int(np.argmax(self.env.belief)) 47: return self.env.step(1 + chosen_idx) 1: """Shared utilities for pipeline scripts. 2: 3: Provides config loading, JSON serialization, MC question deserialization, 4: and path constants used across all pipeline scripts (build, baseline, train, 5: evaluate). 6: 7: Ported from qb-rl reference implementation with import path adaptations 8: for the unified qanta-buzzer codebase. 9: """ 10: 11: from __future__ import annotations 12: 13: import json 14: from dataclasses import asdict, is_dataclass 15: from pathlib import Path 16: from typing import Any 17: 18: from models.likelihoods import LikelihoodModel, build_likelihood_from_config 19: from qb_data.config import load_config as load_yaml_config 20: from qb_data.mc_builder import MCQuestion 21: 22: PROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1] 23: DEFAULT_CONFIG = PROJECT_ROOT / "configs" / "default.yaml" 24: ARTIFACT_DIR = PROJECT_ROOT / "artifacts" 25: 26: 27: def load_config(config_path: str | None = None, smoke: bool = False) -> dict[str, Any]: 28: """Load YAML configuration from a file path. 29: 30: Parameters 31: ---------- 32: config_path : str or None 33: Path to YAML config file. If None, loads ``configs/default.yaml``. 34: 35: Returns 36: ------- 37: dict[str, Any] 38: Parsed config dict with nested structure (data, likelihood, 39: environment, ppo, etc.). 40: """ 41: return load_yaml_config(config_path, smoke=smoke) 42: 43: 44: def build_likelihood_model(config: dict[str, Any], mc_questions: list[MCQuestion]): 45: """Build a likelihood model with shared TF-IDF corpus handling.""" 46: corpus = None 47: if config["likelihood"].get("model") == "tfidf": 48: corpus = [q.question for q in mc_questions] + [ 49: profile 50: for question in mc_questions 51: for profile in question.option_profiles 52: ] 53: return build_likelihood_from_config(config, corpus_texts=corpus) 54: 55: 56: def ensure_dir(path: str | Path) -> Path: 57: """Create a directory (and parents) if it does not exist. 58: 59: Parameters 60: ---------- 61: path : str or Path 62: Directory path to create. 63: 64: Returns 65: ------- 66: Path 67: The created (or existing) directory path. 68: """ 69: p = Path(path) 70: p.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) 71: return p 72: 73: 74: def to_serializable(item: Any) -> Any: 75: """Recursively convert dataclasses to dicts for JSON serialization. 76: 77: Parameters 78: ---------- 79: item : Any 80: Object to convert. Dataclasses are converted via ``asdict()``, 81: dicts and lists are processed recursively. 82: 83: Returns 84: ------- 85: Any 86: JSON-serializable version of the input. 87: """ 88: if is_dataclass(item): 89: return asdict(item) 90: if isinstance(item, dict): 91: return {k: to_serializable(v) for k, v in item.items()} 92: if isinstance(item, list): 93: return [to_serializable(v) for v in item] 94: return item 95: 96: 97: def save_json(path: str | Path, data: Any) -> Path: 98: """Save data to a JSON file, creating parent directories as needed. 99: 100: Applies ``to_serializable`` to convert dataclasses before writing. 101: 102: Parameters 103: ---------- 104: path : str or Path 105: Output file path. 106: data : Any 107: Data to serialize. Dataclasses are converted to dicts automatically. 108: 109: Returns 110: ------- 111: Path 112: The path where the JSON was written. 113: """ 114: p = Path(path) 115: p.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) 116: with p.open("w", encoding="utf-8") as f: 117: json.dump(to_serializable(data), f, indent=2) 118: return p 119: 120: 121: def load_json(path: str | Path) -> Any: 122: """Load data from a JSON file. 123: 124: Parameters 125: ---------- 126: path : str or Path 127: Path to JSON file. 128: 129: Returns 130: ------- 131: Any 132: Parsed JSON data. 133: """ 134: with Path(path).open("r", encoding="utf-8") as f: 135: return json.load(f) 136: 137: 138: def mc_question_from_dict(row: dict[str, Any]) -> MCQuestion: 139: """Reconstruct an MCQuestion dataclass from a JSON-deserialized dict. 140: 141: Parameters 142: ---------- 143: row : dict[str, Any] 144: Dictionary with all MCQuestion fields. 145: 146: Returns 147: ------- 148: MCQuestion 149: Reconstructed MCQuestion instance. 150: """ 151: return MCQuestion( 152: qid=row["qid"], 153: question=row["question"], 154: tokens=list(row["tokens"]), 155: answer_primary=row["answer_primary"], 156: clean_answers=list(row["clean_answers"]), 157: run_indices=list(row["run_indices"]), 158: human_buzz_positions=row.get("human_buzz_positions"), 159: category=row.get("category", ""), 160: cumulative_prefixes=list(row["cumulative_prefixes"]), 161: options=list(row["options"]), 162: gold_index=int(row["gold_index"]), 163: option_profiles=list(row["option_profiles"]), 164: option_answer_primary=list(row["option_answer_primary"]), 165: distractor_strategy=row.get("distractor_strategy", "unknown"), 166: ) 167: 168: 169: def load_mc_questions(path: str | Path) -> list[MCQuestion]: 170: """Load and deserialize a list of MCQuestions from a JSON file. 171: 172: Parameters 173: ---------- 174: path : str or Path 175: Path to JSON file containing a list of serialized MCQuestion dicts. 176: 177: Returns 178: ------- 179: list[MCQuestion] 180: List of reconstructed MCQuestion instances. 181: """ 182: raw = load_json(path) 183: return [mc_question_from_dict(item) for item in raw] 184: 185: 186: # ------------------------------------------------------------------ # 187: # Embedding cache persistence helpers 188: # ------------------------------------------------------------------ # 189: 190: 191: def embedding_cache_path(config: dict[str, Any]) -> Path: 192: """Return the resolved embedding cache file path from config. 193: 194: Uses ``config['likelihood']['cache_dir']`` (default ``'cache/embeddings'``) 195: and appends ``'embedding_cache.npz'``. 196: 197: Parameters 198: ---------- 199: config : dict 200: Full YAML config dict. 201: 202: Returns 203: ------- 204: Path 205: Absolute path to the embedding cache ``.npz`` file. 206: """ 207: cache_dir = config.get("likelihood", {}).get("cache_dir", "cache/embeddings") 208: return PROJECT_ROOT / cache_dir / "embedding_cache.npz" 209: 210: 211: def load_embedding_cache(model: LikelihoodModel, config: dict[str, Any]) -> None: 212: """Load persisted embedding cache into model if file exists. 213: 214: Parameters 215: ---------- 216: model : LikelihoodModel 217: Likelihood model whose embedding_cache will be populated. 218: config : dict 219: Full YAML config dict (used to resolve cache path). 220: """ 221: path = embedding_cache_path(config) 222: n = model.load_cache(path) 223: if n > 0: 224: print(f"Loaded {n} cached embeddings from {path}") 225: 226: 227: def save_embedding_cache(model: LikelihoodModel, config: dict[str, Any]) -> None: 228: """Persist model's embedding cache to disk. 229: 230: Parameters 231: ---------- 232: model : LikelihoodModel 233: Likelihood model whose embedding_cache will be saved. 234: config : dict 235: Full YAML config dict (used to resolve cache path). 236: """ 237: path = embedding_cache_path(config) 238: n = model.save_cache(path) 239: if n > 0: 240: print(f"Saved {n} embeddings to {path}") 1: #!/usr/bin/env python3 2: """ 3: Build multiple-choice dataset from QANTA quiz bowl questions. 4: 5: This script orchestrates the complete data pipeline: 6: 1. Load questions from CSV or HuggingFace 7: 2. Build answer profiles from training data 8: 3. Generate MC questions with anti-artifact guards 9: 4. Create stratified train/val/test splits 10: 5. Save processed datasets as JSON 11: 12: Usage: 13: python scripts/build_mc_dataset.py 14: python scripts/build_mc_dataset.py --smoke # Quick test with 50 questions in artifacts/smoke 15: python scripts/build_mc_dataset.py --config configs/custom.yaml 16: python scripts/build_mc_dataset.py --data.K=5 --data.distractor_strategy=tfidf_profile 17: """ 18: 19: import argparse 20: import json 21: import sys 22: import time 23: from pathlib import Path 24: from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Tuple 25: 26: # Add parent directory to path for imports 27: sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).parent.parent)) 28: 29: from qb_data import TossupQuestion 30: from qb_data.answer_profiles import AnswerProfileBuilder 31: from qb_data.config import load_config, merge_overrides, resolve_data_loading_options 32: from qb_data.data_loader import QANTADatasetLoader 33: from qb_data.dataset_splits import create_stratified_splits 34: from qb_data.huggingface_loader import load_from_huggingface 35: from qb_data.mc_builder import MCBuilder, MCQuestion 36: 37: DEFAULT_OUTPUT_DIR = Path("data/processed") 38: SMOKE_OUTPUT_DIR = Path("artifacts/smoke") 39: 40: 41: def _parse_value(value: str) -> Any: 42: """Parse a CLI override value string into a typed Python value. 43: 44: Tries JSON first, then bool/int/float, and falls back to str. 45: """ 46: try: 47: return json.loads(value) 48: except json.JSONDecodeError: 49: pass 50: if value.lower() == "true": 51: return True 52: if value.lower() == "false": 53: return False 54: if value.lstrip("-").isdigit(): 55: return int(value) 56: try: 57: return float(value) 58: except ValueError: 59: return value 60: 61: 62: def parse_overrides(args: argparse.Namespace) -> Dict[str, Any]: 63: """Parse CLI override arguments into flat dotted-key overrides. 64: 65: Returns a dict with dotted keys (e.g. ``{"data.K": 5}``) that 66: ``merge_overrides`` can apply leaf-by-leaf without clobbering 67: sibling config entries. 68: 69: Parameters 70: ---------- 71: args : argparse.Namespace 72: Parsed CLI arguments. Positional ``overrides`` are 73: ``key=value`` strings where *key* uses dot-notation 74: (e.g. ``data.K=5``). 75: 76: Returns 77: ------- 78: Dict[str, Any] 79: Flat dotted-key overrides ready for ``merge_overrides()``. 80: """ 81: overrides: Dict[str, Any] = {} 82: if hasattr(args, "overrides") and args.overrides: 83: for token in args.overrides: 84: if "=" not in token: 85: continue 86: key, value = token.split("=", 1) 87: overrides[key] = _parse_value(value) 88: return overrides 89: 90: 91: def resolve_output_dir(output_dir: Optional[str], smoke: bool) -> Path: 92: """Resolve the dataset output directory from CLI inputs.""" 93: if output_dir is not None: 94: return Path(output_dir) 95: return SMOKE_OUTPUT_DIR if smoke else DEFAULT_OUTPUT_DIR 96: 97: 98: def parse_args(argv: Optional[list[str]] = None) -> argparse.Namespace: 99: """Parse CLI arguments for dataset construction.""" 100: parser = argparse.ArgumentParser( 101: description="Build multiple-choice dataset from QANTA questions", 102: formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter, 103: epilog=__doc__, 104: ) 105: 106: parser.add_argument( 107: '--config', 108: type=str, 109: default=None, 110: help=( 111: "Path to YAML configuration file. Defaults to configs/default.yaml, " 112: "or the smoke config path selected by load_config() when --smoke is set." 113: ), 114: ) 115: parser.add_argument( 116: '--smoke', 117: action='store_true', 118: help='Use smoke test settings (50 questions, quick run, outputs to artifacts/smoke by default).', 119: ) 120: parser.add_argument( 121: '--output-dir', 122: type=str, 123: default=None, 124: help='Directory to save processed datasets. Defaults to data/processed, or artifacts/smoke when --smoke is set.', 125: ) 126: parser.add_argument( 127: 'overrides', 128: nargs='*', 129: help='Config overrides in format: data.K=5 data.distractor_strategy=tfidf_profile', 130: ) 131: 132: return parser.parse_args(argv) 133: 134: 135: def save_json(path: Path, data: List[Any]) -> None: 136: """ 137: Save dataclass objects to JSON file. 138: 139: Parameters 140: ---------- 141: path : Path 142: Output file path 143: data : List[Any] 144: List of dataclass objects (TossupQuestion or MCQuestion) 145: """ 146: path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) 147: 148: # Convert dataclasses to dictionaries 149: if data and hasattr(data[0], '__dataclass_fields__'): 150: # It's a dataclass, use asdict 151: from dataclasses import asdict 152: json_data = [asdict(item) for item in data] 153: else: 154: json_data = data 155: 156: with open(path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f: 157: json.dump(json_data, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) 158: 159: print(f"Saved {len(data)} items to {path}") 160: 161: 162: def print_statistics( 163: train: List[MCQuestion], 164: val: List[MCQuestion], 165: test: List[MCQuestion], 166: profile_builder: Optional[AnswerProfileBuilder] = None, 167: mc_builder: Optional[MCBuilder] = None 168: ) -> None: 169: """ 170: Print dataset statistics. 171: 172: Parameters 173: ---------- 174: train : List[MCQuestion] 175: Training split 176: val : List[MCQuestion] 177: Validation split 178: test : List[MCQuestion] 179: Test split 180: profile_builder : Optional[AnswerProfileBuilder] 181: Answer profile builder for profile stats 182: mc_builder : Optional[MCBuilder] 183: MC builder for guard rejection stats 184: """ 185: print("\n" + "="*60) 186: print("Dataset Construction Complete") 187: print("="*60) 188: 189: # Split statistics 190: total = len(train) + len(val) + len(test) 191: print(f"\nTotal MC questions: {total}") 192: print(f" Train: {len(train)} ({100*len(train)/total:.1f}%)") 193: print(f" Val: {len(val)} ({100*len(val)/total:.1f}%)") 194: print(f" Test: {len(test)} ({100*len(test)/total:.1f}%)") 195: 196: # Category distribution 197: def get_categories(questions): 198: return set(q.category for q in questions if q.category) 199: 200: all_categories = get_categories(train) | get_categories(val) | get_categories(test) 201: print(f"\nCategories: {len(all_categories)}") 202: 203: # Sample categories 204: sample_cats = sorted(all_categories)[:5] 205: print("Sample categories:", ", ".join(sample_cats)) 206: 207: # Answer profile statistics 208: if profile_builder and hasattr(profile_builder, '_grouped'): 209: print(f"\nAnswer profiles: {len(profile_builder._grouped)}") 210: # Get average questions per answer 211: avg_questions = sum(len(items) for items in profile_builder._grouped.values()) / len(profile_builder._grouped) 212: print(f"Average questions per answer: {avg_questions:.1f}") 213: 214: # Guard rejection statistics 215: if mc_builder and hasattr(mc_builder, 'guard_stats'): 216: stats = mc_builder.guard_stats 217: if stats: 218: print("\nGuard rejection statistics:") 219: for guard_name, count in stats.items(): 220: print(f" {guard_name}: {count} rejections") 221: 222: # Sample MC question 223: if train: 224: sample = train[0] 225: print(f"\nSample MC question:") 226: # Get first sentence from the question 227: first_sentence = sample.question[:100] + "..." if len(sample.question) > 100 else sample.question 228: print(f" Question: {first_sentence}") 229: print(f" Correct answer: {sample.answer_primary}") 230: print(f" Options: {', '.join(sample.options[:3])}...") 231: print(f" Category: {sample.category}") 232: 233: 234: def main(argv: Optional[list[str]] = None): 235: """Main entry point for dataset construction.""" 236: args = parse_args(argv) 237: 238: # Start timing 239: start_time = time.time() 240: 241: # Load configuration 242: print("Loading configuration...") 243: config = load_config(args.config, smoke=args.smoke) 244: 245: # Apply overrides 246: overrides = parse_overrides(args) 247: if overrides: 248: print(f"Applying overrides: {overrides}") 249: config = merge_overrides(config, overrides) 250: 251: # Create output directory 252: output_dir = resolve_output_dir(args.output_dir, smoke=args.smoke) 253: output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) 254: 255: # Load questions 256: print("\nLoading questions...") 257: questions = None 258: data_opts = resolve_data_loading_options(config, smoke=args.smoke) 259: 260: # Try CSV first 261: csv_path = data_opts.get('csv_path') 262: if csv_path and Path(csv_path).exists(): 263: print(f"Loading from CSV: {csv_path}") 264: loader = QANTADatasetLoader() 265: questions = loader.load_from_csv(csv_path) 266: print(f"Loaded {len(questions)} questions from CSV") 267: 268: # Fallback to HuggingFace if configured 269: if questions is None and data_opts.get('use_huggingface'): 270: print("CSV not found, falling back to HuggingFace") 271: dataset_name = data_opts.get('dataset') or 'qanta-challenge/acf-co24-tossups' 272: questions = load_from_huggingface( 273: dataset_name, 274: config_name=data_opts.get('dataset_config'), 275: split=data_opts.get('split', 'eval'), 276: ) 277: print(f"Loaded {len(questions)} questions from HuggingFace") 278: 279: if questions is None: 280: raise FileNotFoundError(f"Could not load questions from {csv_path} and HuggingFace fallback not enabled") 281: 282: # Apply configured limit after loading 283: max_questions = data_opts.get('max_questions') 284: if max_questions is not None and len(questions) > int(max_questions): 285: print(f"Limiting dataset to {int(max_questions)} questions") 286: questions = questions[: int(max_questions)] 287: 288: # Build answer profiles 289: print("\nBuilding answer profiles...") 290: profile_builder = AnswerProfileBuilder( 291: max_tokens_per_profile=config['answer_profiles']['max_tokens_per_profile'], 292: min_questions_per_answer=config['answer_profiles']['min_questions_per_answer'] 293: ) 294: profile_builder.fit(questions) 295: print(f"Built {len(profile_builder._grouped)} answer profiles") 296: 297: # Construct MC questions with guards 298: print("\nConstructing MC questions...") 299: mc_builder = MCBuilder( 300: K=config['data']['K'], 301: strategy=config['data']['distractor_strategy'], 302: embedding_model=config['likelihood'].get( 303: 'sbert_name', 304: config['likelihood'].get('embedding_model', 'all-MiniLM-L6-v2'), 305: ), 306: openai_model=config['likelihood'].get('openai_model', 'text-embedding-3-small'), 307: **config['mc_guards'] 308: ) 309: 310: # Track guard statistics 311: mc_builder.guard_stats = {} 312: 313: mc_questions = mc_builder.build(questions, profile_builder) 314: print(f"Generated {len(mc_questions)} MC questions") 315: 316: if len(mc_questions) < len(questions): 317: print(f"Note: {len(questions) - len(mc_questions)} questions filtered by guards") 318: 319: # Create stratified splits 320: print("\nCreating stratified splits...") 321: ratios = [ 322: config['data']['train_ratio'], 323: config['data']['val_ratio'], 324: config['data']['test_ratio'] 325: ] 326: 327: train, val, test = create_stratified_splits(mc_questions, ratios=ratios) 328: 329: # Save datasets 330: print("\nSaving datasets...") 331: save_json(output_dir / "mc_dataset.json", mc_questions) 332: save_json(output_dir / "train_dataset.json", train) 333: save_json(output_dir / "val_dataset.json", val) 334: save_json(output_dir / "test_dataset.json", test) 335: 336: # Save answer profiles for debugging 337: if profile_builder._grouped: 338: profiles_dict = { 339: answer: { 340: 'question_count': len(items), 341: 'sample_qids': [qid for qid, _ in items[:5]] # First 5 question IDs 342: } 343: for answer, items in profile_builder._grouped.items() 344: } 345: with open(output_dir / "answer_profiles.json", 'w') as f: 346: json.dump(profiles_dict, f, indent=2) 347: print(f"Saved answer profiles to {output_dir / 'answer_profiles.json'}") 348: 349: # Print statistics 350: print_statistics(train, val, test, profile_builder, mc_builder) 351: 352: # Print timing 353: elapsed = time.time() - start_time 354: print(f"\nTotal time: {elapsed:.1f} seconds") 355: 356: if args.smoke: 357: # Print sample MC questions for verification 358: print("\n" + "="*60) 359: print("Sample MC Questions (Smoke Test)") 360: print("="*60) 361: 362: for i, q in enumerate(train[:3], 1): 363: print(f"\nQuestion {i}:") 364: # Get first clue from cumulative_prefixes if available 365: if q.cumulative_prefixes: 366: first_clue = q.cumulative_prefixes[0][:100] + "..." if len(q.cumulative_prefixes[0]) > 100 else q.cumulative_prefixes[0] 367: else: 368: first_clue = q.question[:100] + "..." if len(q.question) > 100 else q.question 369: print(f" First clue: {first_clue}") 370: print(f" Category: {q.category}") 371: print(f" Correct: {q.answer_primary}") 372: print(f" Options: {', '.join(q.options[:3])}...") 373: 374: print("\nDataset construction complete!") 375: return 0 376: 377: 378: if __name__ == '__main__': 379: sys.exit(main()) 1: #!/usr/bin/env python3 2: """ 3: Compare T5-as-likelihood (MLP policy) vs T5-as-policy (end-to-end). 4: 5: Evaluates both approaches on the same test set using the same metric 6: functions (accuracy, S_q, ECE, Brier score, buzz position). 7: 8: **Important caveats for numeric comparison:** 9: 10: The two evaluation paths are *not* fully apples-to-apples: 11: 12: - The MLP path uses config-driven environment settings (e.g. wait_penalty 13: from default.yaml or smoke.yaml). 14: - The T5 path uses its own hardcoded reward settings (wait_penalty=0.1, 15: matching the T5 pipeline's default). 16: - The MLP path builds TF-IDF from test questions + all option profiles. 17: The T5 path builds TF-IDF from profiles of the first 100 questions 18: only (lightweight env reward computation — the T5 policy does not 19: consume TF-IDF likelihoods). 20: - S_q semantics differ: for MLP, c_trace is a sigmoid confidence proxy 21: over belief max; for T5, c_trace is the wait-head buzz probability. 22: 23: These differences are inherent to the two architectures. Accuracy and 24: buzz-position comparisons are directly meaningful. ECE and Brier are 25: computed identically (both use top_p at buzz time). S_q and reward 26: comparisons should be interpreted qualitatively. 27: 28: MLP Policy (Phase 4): 29: T5/TF-IDF computes likelihood scores -> belief features -> MLP 30: policy decides. Uses SB3 PPO with belief-feature observations. 31: 32: T5 Policy (Phase 6): 33: T5 encoder processes text directly -> PolicyHead decides. 34: Uses custom PPO with text observations via TextObservationWrapper. 35: 36: Usage: 37: python scripts/compare_policies.py \\ 38: --mlp-checkpoint checkpoints/ppo/best_model \\ 39: --t5-checkpoint checkpoints/ppo_t5/best_model \\ 40: --output results/t5_comparison.json 41: 42: python scripts/compare_policies.py \\ 43: --t5-checkpoint checkpoints/ppo_t5/best_model \\ 44: --t5-only 45: """ 46: 47: from __future__ import annotations 48: 49: import argparse 50: import json 51: import sys 52: from pathlib import Path 53: from typing import Any 54: 55: PROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1] 56: if str(PROJECT_ROOT) not in sys.path: 57: sys.path.insert(0, str(PROJECT_ROOT)) 58: 59: import numpy as np 60: 61: from evaluation.metrics import ( 62: expected_calibration_error, 63: brier_score, 64: summarize_buzz_metrics, 65: system_score, 66: ) 67: from scripts._common import ARTIFACT_DIR, load_config, load_mc_questions, save_json 68: 69: 70: def evaluate_mlp_policy( 71: checkpoint_path: str, 72: test_questions: list, 73: config: dict, 74: ) -> dict[str, Any]: 75: """Evaluate Phase 4 MLP policy with T5/TF-IDF likelihood on belief features. 76: 77: Loads a PPOBuzzer from an SB3 checkpoint, runs deterministic episodes 78: on each test question, and computes accuracy, S_q, ECE, and buzz 79: position metrics. 80: 81: Parameters 82: ---------- 83: checkpoint_path : str 84: Path to SB3 PPO model checkpoint (`.zip`` file). 85: test_questions : list 86: List of MCQuestion instances to evaluate on. 87: config : dict 88: YAML config dict with environment, likelihood, and data sections. 89: 90: Returns 91: ------- 92: dict[str, Any] 93: Evaluation results: accuracy, mean_sq, ece, brier, avg_buzz_pos, 94: n_questions. 95: """ 96: from agents.ppo_buzzer import PPOBuzzer 97: from models.likelihoods import TfIdfLikelihood 98: from qb_env.tossup_env import make_env_from_config 99: 100: # Build likelihood model 101: corpus = ( 102: [q.question for q in test_questions] 103: + [p for q in test_questions for p in q.option_profiles] 104: ) 105: likelihood_model = TfIdfLikelihood(corpus_texts=corpus) 106: 107: # Build environment with all test questions 108: env = make_env_from_config( 109: mc_questions=test_questions, 110: likelihood_model=likelihood_model, 111: config=config, 112: ) 113: 114: # Load trained agent 115: agent = PPOBuzzer.load(checkpoint_path, env=env) 116: 117: # Run episodes 118: results = [] 119: for _ in range(len(test_questions)): 120: trace = agent.run_episode(deterministic=True) 121: results.append(trace) 122: 123: # Compute metrics 124: buzz_metrics = summarize_buzz_metrics(results) 125: 126: # Extract confidences and outcomes for calibration — use top_p 127: from dataclasses import asdict 128: 129: rows = [asdict(r) for r in results] 130: confidences = [] 131: outcomes = [] 132: buzz_positions = [] 133: for row in rows: 134: top_p_trace = list(row.get("top_p_trace", [])) 135: c_trace = list(row.get("c_trace", [])) 136: conf_trace = top_p_trace if top_p_trace else c_trace 137: buzz_step = int(row.get("buzz_step", max(0, len(conf_trace) - 1))) 138: if conf_trace: 139: idx = min(max(0, buzz_step), len(conf_trace) - 1) 140: confidences.append(float(conf_trace[idx])) 141: outcomes.append(1 if bool(row.get("correct", False)) else 0) 142: buzz_positions.append(buzz_step) 143: 144: ece = expected_calibration_error(confidences, outcomes) 145: brier = brier_score(confidences, outcomes) 146: 147: return { 148: "accuracy": buzz_metrics["buzz_accuracy"], 149: "mean_sq": buzz_metrics["mean_sq"], 150: "ece": ece, 151: "brier": brier, 152: "avg_buzz_pos": float(np.mean(buzz_positions)) if buzz_positions else 0.0, 153: "mean_reward": buzz_metrics["mean_reward_like"], 154: "n_questions": len(test_questions), 155: } 156: 157: 158: def evaluate_t5_policy( 159: checkpoint_path: str, 160: test_questions: list, 161: config: dict, 162: ) -> dict[str, Any]: 163: """Evaluate Phase 6 T5 end-to-end policy on text observations. 164: 165: Loads a T5PolicyModel from checkpoint, runs deterministic episodes 166: on each test question using TextObservationWrapper, and computes the 167: same metrics as evaluate_mlp_policy for fair comparison. 168: 169: Parameters 170: ---------- 171: checkpoint_path : str 172: Path to T5PolicyModel checkpoint directory. 173: test_questions : list 174: List of MCQuestion instances to evaluate on. 175: config : dict 176: YAML config dict. 177: 178: Returns 179: ------- 180: dict[str, Any] 181: Evaluation results: accuracy, mean_sq, ece, brier, avg_buzz_pos, 182: n_questions. 183: """ 184: import torch 185: from models.t5_policy import T5PolicyModel 186: from models.likelihoods import TfIdfLikelihood 187: from qb_env.text_wrapper import TextObservationWrapper 188: from qb_env.tossup_env import TossupMCEnv 189: 190: # Load T5 policy model 191: model = T5PolicyModel.load_pretrained(checkpoint_path, device="cpu") 192: model.eval() 193: 194: # Build lightweight likelihood for environment reward computation 195: corpus = [] 196: for q in test_questions[:100]: 197: corpus.extend(q.option_profiles) 198: likelihood_model = TfIdfLikelihood(corpus_texts=corpus) 199: 200: correct_count = 0 201: total_count = 0 202: sq_scores = [] 203: confidences = [] 204: outcomes = [] 205: buzz_positions = [] 206: 207: with torch.no_grad(): 208: for question in test_questions: 209: env = TossupMCEnv( 210: questions=[question], 211: likelihood_model=likelihood_model, 212: K=len(question.options), 213: reward_mode="time_penalty", 214: wait_penalty=0.1, 215: belief_mode="from_scratch", 216: ) 217: wrapped_env = TextObservationWrapper(env) 218: 219: obs, info = wrapped_env.reset() 220: done = False 221: c_trace = [] 222: g_trace = [] 223: top_p_trace = [] 224: episode_reward = 0.0 225: step_count = 0 226: 227: while not done: 228: inputs = model.tokenizer( 229: obs, 230: return_tensors="pt", 231: padding=True, 232: truncation=True, 233: max_length=512, 234: ) 235: actions, act_info = model.select_action( 236: inputs["input_ids"], 237: inputs["attention_mask"], 238: deterministic=True, 239: ) 240: 241: action = actions.item() 242: 243: wait_probs = act_info["wait_probs"] 244: buzz_prob = wait_probs[0, 1].item() 245: c_trace.append(buzz_prob) 246: 247: answer_probs = act_info["answer_probs"] 248: gold_prob = answer_probs[0, question.gold_index].item() 249: g_trace.append(gold_prob) 250: 251: top_p = float(answer_probs[0].max().item()) 252: top_p_trace.append(top_p) 253: 254: obs, reward, terminated, truncated, step_info = ( 255: wrapped_env.step(action) 256: ) 257: done = terminated or truncated 258: episode_reward += reward 259: step_count += 1 260: 261: sq = system_score(c_trace, g_trace) 262: sq_scores.append(sq) 263: 264: is_correct = step_info.get("correct", False) or step_info.get( 265: "forced_correct", False 266: ) 267: if is_correct: 268: correct_count += 1 269: total_count += 1 270: 271: # Calibration: use top_p (max answer prob) for consistency 272: # with belief-feature agents 273: if top_p_trace: 274: buzz_step = step_count - 1 275: confidences.append(top_p_trace[-1]) 276: outcomes.append(1 if is_correct else 0) 277: buzz_positions.append(buzz_step) 278: 279: accuracy = correct_count / max(1, total_count) 280: mean_sq = float(np.mean(sq_scores)) if sq_scores else 0.0 281: ece = expected_calibration_error(confidences, outcomes) 282: brier_val = brier_score(confidences, outcomes) 283: avg_buzz_pos = float(np.mean(buzz_positions)) if buzz_positions else 0.0 284: 285: return { 286: "accuracy": accuracy, 287: "mean_sq": mean_sq, 288: "ece": ece, 289: "brier": brier_val, 290: "avg_buzz_pos": avg_buzz_pos, 291: "mean_reward": 0.0, # Not tracked per-episode for T5 policy eval 292: "n_questions": total_count, 293: } 294: 295: 296: def print_comparison( 297: mlp_results: dict[str, Any] | None, 298: t5_results: dict[str, Any], 299: test_size: int, 300: ) -> dict[str, Any]: 301: """Print and return comparison summary. 302: 303: Parameters 304: ---------- 305: mlp_results : dict or None 306: MLP policy evaluation results. None if --t5-only. 307: t5_results : dict 308: T5 policy evaluation results. 309: test_size : int 310: Number of test questions evaluated. 311: 312: Returns 313: ------- 314: dict[str, Any] 315: Complete comparison dict for JSON serialization. 316: """ 317: print("\n" + "=" * 70) 318: print("COMPARISON RESULTS: T5-as-Likelihood vs T5-as-Policy") 319: print("=" * 70) 320: print(f"Test set size: {test_size}") 321: print() 322: 323: if mlp_results is not None: 324: print(f"{'Metric':<20} {'MLP (T5-likelihood)':>20} {'T5 (end-to-end)':>20} {'Difference':>15}") 325: print("-" * 75) 326: for metric in ["accuracy", "mean_sq", "ece", "brier", "avg_buzz_pos"]: 327: mlp_val = mlp_results.get(metric, 0.0) 328: t5_val = t5_results.get(metric, 0.0) 329: diff = t5_val - mlp_val 330: print(f"{metric:<20} {mlp_val:>20.4f} {t5_val:>20.4f} {diff:>+15.4f}") 331: else: 332: print("T5 Policy (end-to-end) results:") 333: print("-" * 40) 334: for metric in ["accuracy", "mean_sq", "ece", "brier", "avg_buzz_pos"]: 335: val = t5_results.get(metric, 0.0) 336: print(f" {metric:<20}: {val:.4f}") 337: 338: # Build comparison dict 339: comparison: dict[str, Any] = { 340: "test_size": test_size, 341: "t5_policy": t5_results, 342: } 343: if mlp_results is not None: 344: comparison["mlp_policy"] = mlp_results 345: comparison["difference"] = { 346: metric: t5_results.get(metric, 0.0) - mlp_results.get(metric, 0.0) 347: for metric in ["accuracy", "mean_sq", "ece", "brier", "avg_buzz_pos"] 348: } 349: 350: return comparison 351: 352: 353: def parse_compare_args() -> argparse.Namespace: 354: """Parse comparison script arguments. 355: 356: Returns 357: ------- 358: argparse.Namespace 359: Parsed arguments. 360: """ 361: parser = argparse.ArgumentParser( 362: description="Compare T5-as-likelihood (MLP) vs T5-as-policy.", 363: ) 364: parser.add_argument( 365: "--mlp-checkpoint", 366: type=str, 367: default=None, 368: help="Path to Phase 4 MLP policy checkpoint.", 369: ) 370: parser.add_argument( 371: "--t5-checkpoint", 372: type=str, 373: required=True, 374: help="Path to Phase 6 T5 policy checkpoint.", 375: ) 376: parser.add_argument( 377: "--config", 378: type=str, 379: default=None, 380: help="Path to YAML config file.", 381: ) 382: parser.add_argument( 383: "--mc-path", 384: type=str, 385: default=None, 386: help="Path to MC dataset JSON file.", 387: ) 388: parser.add_argument( 389: "--output", 390: type=str, 391: default="results/t5_comparison.json", 392: help="Path for output JSON results.", 393: ) 394: parser.add_argument( 395: "--smoke", 396: action="store_true", 397: help="Quick test with first 50 questions.", 398: ) 399: parser.add_argument( 400: "--t5-only", 401: action="store_true", 402: help="Only evaluate T5 policy (skip MLP comparison).", 403: ) 404: return parser.parse_args() 405: 406: 407: def main() -> None: 408: """Run the comparison experiment.""" 409: args = parse_compare_args() 410: 411: # Load config 412: config = load_config(args.config) 413: 414: # Load test questions 415: if args.mc_path: 416: mc_path = Path(args.mc_path) 417: else: 418: candidates = [ 419: ARTIFACT_DIR / "main" / "mc_dataset.json", 420: ARTIFACT_DIR / "smoke" / "mc_dataset.json", 421: PROJECT_ROOT / "data" / "processed" / "mc_dataset.json", 422: ] 423: mc_path = None 424: for candidate in candidates: 425: if candidate.exists(): 426: mc_path = candidate 427: break 428: if mc_path is None: 429: print("ERROR: No MC dataset found. Run build_mc_dataset.py first.") 430: sys.exit(1) 431: 432: print(f"Loading questions from: {mc_path}") 433: all_questions = load_mc_questions(mc_path) 434: print(f"Loaded {len(all_questions)} questions") 435: 436: # Use last 15% as test set (matching standard split) 437: import random 438: rng = random.Random(42) 439: shuffled = all_questions[:] 440: rng.shuffle(shuffled) 441: test_start = int(len(shuffled) * 0.85) 442: test_questions = shuffled[test_start:] 443: 444: if args.smoke: 445: test_questions = test_questions[:50] 446: 447: print(f"Test set: {len(test_questions)} questions") 448: 449: # Evaluate MLP policy (if checkpoint provided and not t5-only) 450: mlp_results = None 451: if args.mlp_checkpoint and not args.t5_only: 452: print("\n" + "-" * 40) 453: print("Evaluating MLP policy (T5-as-likelihood)...") 454: print("-" * 40) 455: mlp_results = evaluate_mlp_policy( 456: args.mlp_checkpoint, test_questions, config 457: ) 458: print(f" Accuracy: {mlp_results['accuracy']:.4f}") 459: print(f" Mean S_q: {mlp_results['mean_sq']:.4f}") 460: 461: # Evaluate T5 policy 462: print("\n" + "-" * 40) 463: print("Evaluating T5 policy (end-to-end)...") 464: print("-" * 40) 465: t5_results = evaluate_t5_policy( 466: args.t5_checkpoint, test_questions, config 467: ) 468: print(f" Accuracy: {t5_results['accuracy']:.4f}") 469: print(f" Mean S_q: {t5_results['mean_sq']:.4f}") 470: 471: # Print comparison 472: comparison = print_comparison(mlp_results, t5_results, len(test_questions)) 473: 474: # Save results 475: output_path = Path(args.output) 476: output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) 477: save_json(output_path, comparison) 478: print(f"\nResults saved to {output_path}") 479: 480: 481: if __name__ == "__main__": 482: main() 1: #!/usr/bin/env bash 2: # Manual smoke pipeline -- runs the four-stage belief-feature smoke workflow. 3: # Intended for human verification, not CI (stages are heavyweight ML runs). 4: # 5: # Prereqs: pip install -e . Check artifacts/smoke/ ===" 1: #!/usr/bin/env python3 2: """ 3: Train T5 policy with supervised warm-start then PPO fine-tuning. 4: 5: End-to-end pipeline for training a T5PolicyModel on quiz bowl questions: 6: 1. scripts/train_t5_policy.py --config configs/t5_policy.yaml \ 18: --skip-supervised --model-path checkpoints/supervised/best_model 19: 20: # Custom number of PPO iterations 21: python scripts/train_t5_policy.py --config configs/t5_policy.yaml \ 22: --ppo-iterations 50 23: """ 24: 25: from __future__ import annotations 26: 27: import argparse 28: import sys 29: from pathlib import 132: for key, val in smoke["ppo"].items(): 133: config["ppo"][key] = val 134: # Override data settings 135: if "data" in smoke: 136: for key, val in smoke["data"].items(): 137: config["data"][key] = val 138: 139: if args.ppo_iterations is not None: 140: config["ppo"]["iterations"] = args.ppo_iterations 141: 142: return config 143: 144: 145: def flatten_config(config: dict) -> dict: 146: 168: elif hasattr(torch.backends, "mps") and torch.backends.mps.is_available(): 169: device = "mps" 170: else: 171: device = "cpu" 172: flat["device"] = device 173: flat["max_input_length"] = flat["checkpoint_dir"] = sup.get("checkpoint_dir", "checkpoints") 185: 186: # PPO section 187: ppo = config.get("ppo", {}) 188: flat["ppo_lr"] = ppo.get("lr", 1e-5) 189: flat["ppo_iterations"] = ppo.get("iterations", 100) 190: flat["ppo_batch_size"] = ppo.get("batch_size", 8) 191: flat["ppo_epochs_per_iter"] = ppo.get("epochs_per_iter", 4) 192: flat["ppo_gamma"] = ppo.get("gamma", 0.99) 193: flat["ppo_gae_lambda"] = ppo.get("gae_lambda", 0.95) 194: flat["ppo_clip_ratio"] = ppo.get("clip_ratio", 0.2) 195: flat["ppo_value_coef"] = ppo.get("value_coef", 0.5) 196: flat["ppo_entropy_coef"] = ppo.get("entropy_coef", 0.01) 197: flat["ppo_max_grad_norm"] = ppo.get("max_grad_norm", 0.5) 198: flat["ppo_episodes_per_iter"] = ppo.get("episodes_per_iter", 16) 199: flat["eval_interval"] = ppo.get("eval_interval", 10) 200: flat["save_interval"] = ppo.get("save_interval", 20) 201: 202: return flat 203: 204: 205: def load_questions(args: argparse.Namespace, config: dict) -> list: 206: 0.15) 289: 290: rng = random.Random(seed) 291: shuffled = questions[:] 292: rng.shuffle(shuffled) 293: 294: n = len(shuffled) 295: n_train = int(n * train_size) 296: n_val = int(n * val_size) 297: 298: train_questions = shuffled[:n_train] 299: val_questions = shuffled[n_train : n_train + n_val] 300: test_questions = shuffled[n_train + n_val :] 301: 302: print(f"Split: {len(train_questions)} train, {len(val_questions)} val, {len(test_questions)} test") 303: return train_questions, val_questions, test_questions 304: 305: 306: def main() -> None: 307: """Run the full T5 policy training supervised training, using model: {supervised_model_path}") 342: 343: # Phase 2: PPO fine-tuning 344: print("\n" + "=" * 60) 345: print("PHASE 2: PPO FINE-TUNING (T5 Policy)") 346: print("=" * 60)
